read: The new two-stage compact-micro path preserves the query-major dot-K producer, writes compact micro rows, then explicitly merges micro rows into seq-tile compact rows before the final fold. It passes S128/S512 parity and proves the micro-to-seq merge itself is feasible, but it is not a speed path: at S4096 the producer still writes one update per valid selected KxD contribution before the merge, so the added reducer only adds latency.
decision: Do not pursue a separate compact micro-to-seq merge as the next sparse dKV optimization. The merge is under 1 ms at S4096, but it sits after the same raw producer/writeback wall and makes the full path slower than the retained compact-micro partial-store baseline.
next_action: Move the reducer inside the query-major producer itself: lane/warp/block segmented accumulation before micro partial global writes, or fuse LI/compressor dKV so the producer never emits one update per valid selected KxD contribution.
